title: How to Use AI for Lead Follow-Up (Prompts Included)
dek: Deals don't die at showings. They die in the silence afterward. AI fixes the silence, if you use it the right way. Here's my exact system, prompts included.
heroAlt: How to Use AI for Lead Follow-Up (Prompts Included)

blocks.html: I keep a single saved prompt and fill three blanks after every showing: who they are, what they saw, what their hesitation was.

"Write a short, warm follow-up text to a [renter type] who toured a [unit] at [building] today, loved [X], hesitated on [Y]. Confirm I'm [specific next action] tomorrow morning. My voice: professional, brief, no exclamation points, no pressure."
Ten seconds later I have a draft that sounds like me on my best day. I edit one line and send.

blocks.text: Your follow-up should run while you sleep. Your relationships shouldn't.

blocks.html: The revival: "Write a brief check-in to a renter who toured 3 weeks ago and went quiet. New information: [unit's status / new availability]. No guilt, no pressure, lead with the information."
The prep email: "Write a friendly pre-tour email confirming [time] at [building], including this document checklist: [list]. Frame the checklist as making things easy, not as homework."
The anniversary: "Write a warm one-year check-in to a past client, asking how the apartment's treating them, mentioning I'm always happy to help them or friends with a move. Zero sales pressure."

Never send the first draft: read it out loud, and if a sentence doesn't sound like your mouth, fix it. And never automate the moments that matter: the machine drafts, you decide. AI handles the 80 percent that's repetitive. You bring the 20 percent that signs.
One system like this every Saturday: The Lease Up, free, link below. Better agents win.
